content: Remote OK Operations Supervisor Healthcare South Georgia Belle Belle is a fast-growing healthcare company revolutionizing in-home care â starting from the feet up. More than 25 million U.S. seniors can no longer safely care for their feet, contributing to over $38 billion in avoidable medical costs each year.<br><br>Belle trains and deploys a network of Community Health Workers â known as Belle Technicians â to deliver in-home foot exams, preventive care, and care management. Backed by advanced data science, our care model detects emerging health risks early, often before they escalate. When issues arise, our telehealth team and nurses coordinate follow-up care and take action.<br><br>The result? Belle delivers clinical outcomes on par with blockbuster drugs, without a single pill. Our model helps prevent complications, reduce hospital visits, and improve quality of life â all with care that people genuinely love.<br><br>Our mission: Bring Joy to Healthcare. Join us if our cause inspires you!<br><br><strong>About This Role<br><br></strong>As an Operations Supervisor in South Georgia, you'll drive growth, build a strong team, and create community impact in your designated market. This role blends recruitment, market development, and operational excellence, ensuring seniors receive life-changing in-home care.<br><br>You'll be actively engaged in technician development, field shadowing, performance reviews, and issue resolution. You'll also collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to ensure every appointment is delivered with consistency, professionalism, and warmth.<br><br>You will also serve as the local face of the company â building trust with your team, ensuring quality standards are met, and collaborating cross-functionally to help Belle scale successfully. You'll work mainly remote but need to be available in the service area to shadow the field and provide support.<br><br><strong>Key Responsibilities<br><br></strong><strong>Team Leadership &amp; Development<br><br></strong><ul><li>Lead technician hiring and onboarding in partnership with centralized recruiting and training teams</li><li>Conduct field-based shadowing and provide real-time coaching</li><li>Facilitate technician check-ins and performance reviews</li><li>Support technicians in meeting certification and compliance requirements</li><li>Foster a connected and motivated team culture with ongoing engagement initiatives</li><li>Provide proactive coaching on hospitality, documentation, attendance, and professionalism<br><br></li></ul><strong>Operational Oversight<br><br></strong><ul><li>Monitor KPI's such as attendance, appointment fulfillment, and CSAT</li><li>Ensure proper execution of Belle's care model and scope of practice</li><li>Manage documentation audits and follow up on quality concerns</li><li>Track technician availability and support schedule optimization</li><li>Report safety concerns, policy violations, or compliance issues as needed</li><li>Collaborate with HR, Compliance, and Member Experience teams on escalations<br><br></li></ul><strong>Requirements<br><br></strong>Qualifications<br><br><ul><li>Bachelor's degree in Business, Healthcare, or a related field preferred</li><li>Prior experience managing a field-based or remote team (healthcare, retail, or service industries ideal)</li><li>Strong people leadership and coaching abilities</li><li>Highly organized, tech-savvy, and action-oriented</li><li>Excellent communication skills â clear, direct, and compassionate</li><li>Familiarity with scheduling, attendance, or workforce tools is a plus</li><li>Resides in South Georgia and able to travel locally to support CHWs in the field (15-25% expected)<br><br></li></ul><strong>Benefits<br><br></strong><ul><li>Competitive compensation based on experience </li><li>Incentive Bonus paid quarterly</li><li>Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance Benefits</li><li>401k</li><li>PTO, Sick, and Paid Holidays</li><li>Opportunity for significant career growth and expansion of responsibilities</li><li>Ability to reshape an industry and protect Join us.<br> <br><strong>Job Description<br></strong> <br>As a <strong>Construction Inspector</strong> , you will provide on-site inspection, monitoring, reporting and documentation for municipal infrastructure projects, including water/ wastewater treatment facility, water distribution piping, sanitary sewers, and roadway construction. In this role, you will help ensure that work is completed in accordance with approved plans, specifications, and regulatory standards while supporting project managers and engineers throughout the construction process.<br> <br><strong>Responsibilities include:<br></strong> <ul><li>Perform daily field inspections of civil infrastructure construction</li> <li>Monitor contractor activities for compliance with project plans, specifications, and municipal standards.</li> <li>Document daily construction activities, quantities, and site conditions using inspection reports and photo logs.</li> <li>Observe and verify materials, testing procedures, and workmanship.</li> <li>Coordinate with contractors, engineers, utility providers, and municipal staff to resolve field issues.</li> <li>Assist with reviewing shop drawings, RFIs, and change orders as needed.</li> <li>Monitor health and safety compliance on-site</li> <li>Ensure compliance with safety regulations and environmental requirements.
